retry.notes: The station's four round openings are WINDOWS, not eyes — the pen draws them as empty double rings and the light fill colors them as plain amber portholes. Do NOT draw pupils, eyeballs, or catchlight discs inside any of them; leave every window interior empty.

chalk.review: The shipped 2026-07 chalk drew a solid pupil-disc-with-catchlight inside ALL FOUR windows (three head portholes + the big body hatch, pupils glancing sideways) and the night fill painted them as eyeballs — dark mode anthropomorphizes the station while the light side has plain windows. Confirm every window is empty on the chalk-over-night COMPOSITE (ISSUES Tier 1). After a chalk regen, regenerate the night fill and re-punch.
chalk.why: 2026-07-13 dark-mode composite review — the eye-flavored chalk instruction invents eyes on faceless subjects (see objects/house-wide's NO-EYES guard); same class as ship-tall. The eye audit is light-referenced (0 cores), so the invented eyes are invisible to it.

retry.notes: The porthole is a WINDOW, not an eye — the pen has an empty riveted ring and the light fill shows plain glass with a sheen. Do NOT draw a pupil, eyeball, or catchlight disc inside it; leave the window interior empty.

chalk.review: The shipped 2026-07 chalk drew a solid pupil-disc-with-catchlight inside the empty porthole and the night fill painted it as a literal brown eyeball with a white sclera — dark mode anthropomorphizes the rocket. Confirm the porthole is plain glass on the chalk-over-night COMPOSITE (ISSUES Tier 1). After a chalk regen, regenerate the night fill and re-punch.
chalk.why: 2026-07-13 dark-mode composite review — the eye-flavored chalk instruction invents eyes on faceless subjects (see objects/house-wide's NO-EYES guard). Worse than gate-blind: audit-fill-eyes scores the invented eyeball 8/8 lively cores, so the eye machinery actively rewards the defect.
